2604.27217.title: Toward Personalized Digital Twins for Cognitive Decline Assessment: A Multimodal, Uncertainty-Aware Framework
2604.27217.paper_summary: Cognitive decline is highly heterogeneous across individuals, which complicates prognosis, trial design, and treatment planning. We present the Personalized Cognitive Decline Assessment Digital Twin (PCD-DT), a multimodal and uncertainty-aware framework for modeling patient-specific disease trajectories from sparse, noisy, and irregular longitudinal data. The framework combines three methodological components: (1) latent state-space models for individualized temporal dynamics, (2) multimodal fusion for clinical, biomarker, and imaging features, and (3) uncertainty-aware validation and adaptive updating for robust digital twin operation. We also outline how conditional generative models can support data augmentation and stress testing for underrepresented progression patterns. As a preliminary feasibility study, we analyze longitudinal TADPOLE trajectories and show clear separation between cognitively normal and Alzheimer's disease cohorts in ADAS13, ventricle volume, and hippocampal volume over five years. We further conduct a multimodal next-visit prediction ablation using an LSTM sequence model on 3{,}003 visit-pair sequences derived from TADPOLE, where the combined cognitive plus MRI configuration achieves the lowest standardized RMSE for both ADAS13 (0.4419) and ventricle volume (0.5842), outperforming a Last Observation Carried Forward baseline. A Bayesian tensor modeling component for high-dimensional imaging fusion is also discussed. These results support the feasibility of the proposed architecture while also highlighting the need for stronger uncertainty calibration and longer-horizon predictive evaluation. The PCD-DT framework provides a principled starting point for personalized in silico modeling in neurodegenerative disease. This work positions PCD-DT as a foundational step toward clinically deployable, uncertainty-aware digital twin systems.
2604.27217.paper_summary_zh: 認知衰退在個體之間高度異質，這使得預後、試驗設計和治療計劃變得複雜。我們提出了個性化認知衰退評估數位雙胞胎（PCD-DT），這是一個多模態且考慮不確定性的框架，用於從稀疏、嘈雜和不規則的縱向數據中建模患者特定的疾病軌跡。該框架結合了三個方法論組件：（1）用於個性化時間動態的潛在狀態空間模型，（2）用於臨床、生物標記和影像特徵的多模態融合，以及（3）用於穩健數位雙胞胎運作的不確定性驗證和自適應更新。我們還概述了條件生成模型如何支持數據增強和對代表性不足的進展模式進行壓力測試。作為初步可行性研究，我們分析了縱向TADPOLE軌跡，並顯示在ADAS13、腦室體積和海馬體積上，認知正常組與阿茲海默症組之間有明顯的區分，這一過程持續了五年。我們進一步使用LSTM序列模型對來自TADPOLE的3,003對訪問序列進行了多模態下一次訪問預測消融，其中結合的認知加MRI配置在ADAS13（0.4419）和腦室體積（0.5842）上達到了最低的標準化RMSE，超越了最後觀察值延續的基準。一個用於高維影像融合的貝葉斯張量建模組件也被討論。這些結果支持了所提架構的可行性，同時也突顯了對更強的不確定性校準和更長期預測評估的需求。PCD-DT框架為神經退行性疾病中的個性化計算建模提供了一個原則性的起點。這項工作將PCD-DT定位為朝向臨床可部署的、不確定性意識數位雙胞胎系統邁出的基礎性步驟。
2604.27217.author: Bulent Soykan et.al.
2604.27217.authors: Bulent Soykan, Gulsah Hancerliogullari Koksalmis, Hsin-Hsiung Huang, Laura J. Brattain
